$schema: Legacy identifier map — the sole bridge between the numeric-era corpus and the capability specs, and the tombstone registry: every key under requirements is a retired identifier. requirements: legacy numeric id -> {target: current requirement, scenarios: anchors pinning its specific edge cases, note, change}. reviews: legacy review item -> {scenarios: anchors encoding its edge case(s), resolvedBy, note, change}; full item bodies remain in ../review/. Each entry's change names the change that retired the id by its stable, dateless name (an archived change's folder carries a date prefix over the same name). All targets, anchors, and change fields are validated by scripts/check-spec-citations.mjs. The corpus retired in full on 2026-07-24; targets resolve in openspec/specs/ or, until archived, in the named open change's deltas.

provenance.note: Module 01 re-authored by migrate-module-01-game-rules (game-rules/* targets re-prefixed to game-engine/* by the capability rename in mint-global-invariants); module 02's cross-cutting half by mint-global-invariants. The remaining corpus (modules 02-08, 525 identifiers) retired in full by the 2026-07-24 migration train — one change per capability plus extend-global-invariants and a closing sweep of ownerless mechanism/drop tombstones. Per-entry attribution lives in each entry's change field; the train's planning artifacts are archived under ../spec-migration/.

02-REQ-003.note: Exactly one transient SpacetimeDB instance per started game; unstarted games have none. Dedupe cluster with 02-REQ-019/020/021.

02-REQ-005.note: Captain unilaterally nominates the server domain on the team record; no server acceptance. The mid-game freeze half is carried by team-management/roster-freeze (which enumerates nomination changes) and the captain gate by team-management/captain-authority — cited, not re-owned.

02-REQ-006.note: A team joins games only with a nominated server; no pure-human teams — the gate is structural.

02-REQ-009.note: Real-time committed-state sync without per-turn polling — dedupe with 04-REQ-053 into one delivery requirement; graduated from the module-02 parked ledger.

02-REQ-010.note: Invisibility filtered at the data layer, keyed on the querying connection's team; the data-layer-enforcement principle half was already covered by global-invariants/security-enforced-outside-the-library — what retires here is the invisibility feature's own visibility semantics. Graduated from the module-02 parked ledger.

02-REQ-011.note: Last-write-wins per snake within a turn — re-authored as effective-move semantics over the append-only log (latest current-turn entry wins), never destructive overwrite, per the author-resolved staged-moves model (assignment-matrix Q4). Graduated from the module-02 parked ledger; its reconciliation flag is discharged.

02-REQ-012.note: SUPERSEDED: the legacy 'consume and clear staged moves in the resolving transaction' wording was overridden by the later append-only model (retained for the game's lifetime, nothing cleared at resolution, no cancel operation) — author-resolved contradiction, assignment-matrix Q4. The id retires onto the append-only requirement that replaced it; the consumed-at-resolution residue survives only as 'resolution consumes what the log holds at declaration' (#accepted-until-declaration). Graduated from the module-02 parked ledger.

02-REQ-013.note: Complete per-turn record retained in-instance for the game's lifetime, self-sufficient for reconstructing any prior turn without consulting any external system; authored with 04-REQ-004's contents enumeration (dedupe cluster with 04-REQ-004..012).

02-REQ-014.note: No per-turn posting of game state to any external system; the record leaves once, at game end, bundled into the terminal notification (dedupe with 04-REQ-061/065's export half; the parked ledger's review-driven edit — positive once-at-end statement carries the rule — honoured).

02-REQ-016.note: Convex hosts all identity and credential infrastructure — folded into the sole-issuer requirement.

02-REQ-018.note: At most one operator per snake and one snake per operator — dedupe with the two subsystem-level exclusivity ids into one requirement (selection-exclusivity cluster). The Convex-owns-selection authority split had already graduated to global-invariants/team-granularity-authorization. Graduated from the module-02 parked ledger.

02-REQ-019.note: Convex orchestrates provisioning at the moment of launch and teardown after end (the launch-orchestration and teardown-after-persistence requirements carry the two ends of the bracket).

02-REQ-020.note: No instance reuse across games; fresh instance on every creation path (first game, successor, competition round) with no special cases.

02-REQ-021.note: Instance lifetime bounded to its game; torn down after the game ends and the record's persistence is confirmed. The parked ledger's walkover/forward-status draft half is carried by status-authority (#walkover-straight-to-finished); the terminal-state term is settled as finished per that draft's note.

02-REQ-022.note: Convex obtains and persists the complete game record before teardown; the retrieval pattern was deliberately left open (02-REVIEW-006) and later resolved as bundling into the finish notification (finish-notification). What the record must contain for replay sufficiency is replay-and-audit's.

02-REQ-022a.note: Terminal state learned only via a runtime-pushed notification; Convex holds no live gameplay subscription to any instance. Dedupe with 04-REQ-061a and 05-REQ-038.

02-REQ-024.note: server-side Centaur-state write authority via the per-team game credential; credential scoping at identity-and-authorization/game-credential-scope, captain-gated configuration writes at bot-configuration/captain-only-team-configuration

02-REQ-025.note: the hosting server computes for every hosted snake; the automatic-vs-manual split lives at operator-control/manual-mode

02-REQ-027.note: the action-log half lives at replay-and-audit/team-action-log

02-REQ-028.note: operator interface surface — substance owned by module 08

Drive-assignment reads/writes ride the same connection but their semantics belong to the bot-configuration story; the action log to replay-and-audit. 02-REQ-041.note: Spectators connect read-only under a spectator access token, filtered as opponents of every team (02-REVIEW-007). Transport binding (WebSocket) is mechanism — the authored behaviour is transport-neutral. 02-REQ-043.note: Management page limited to identity/nomination/membership (incl. captain transfer and coach designation), never competitive configuration; seam — nomination and healthcheck semantics belong to the team-server-management story.

02-REQ-052.note: Team↔server relationship many-to-many over time; exactly one server per team per game. The operator-trust half of shared hosting is global-invariants/server-trust-boundary (already minted), not re-authored here.

02-REQ-061.note: the no-privileged-deployment half; the open-source licensing stance is design rationale in the archived mint-global-invariants change

02-REQ-063.note: malicious-server trust trade-off — accepted-risk rationale recorded in the archived mint-global-invariants change's design.md; the mechanism it concerns is global-invariants/access-follows-identity

03-REQ-001.note: Two persistent kinds plus derived game-participant identities; kind-distinguishability and no-anonymous-mutators halves are global-invariants (extend-global-invariants change).

03-REQ-002.note: Human = natural person with a Google account, identified by email (03-REVIEW-002/003 upheld).

03-REQ-003.note: Team as persistent competitive unit; nominated server domain is configuration, not identity.

03-REQ-004.note: Game-participant identities as per-game derived connections; role set widened to include coach per the later legacy amendments.

03-REQ-005.note: Identity kinds distinguishable wherever platform code observes them; no code path handles an ambiguous-kind identity.

03-REQ-006.note: No anonymous/unauthenticated participants in any state-mutating role, on any runtime.

03-REQ-007.note: Google OAuth via Convex; persistent session.

03-REQ-008.note: Email canonical; same email merges; provider email change forks a new identity, history stays on the old (03-REVIEW-003).

03-REQ-009.note: No passwords or shared secrets stored for humans.

03-REQ-012.note: No shared secret stored, exchanged, or transmitted with a server at nomination or any other time; game invitations are the sole secret channel.

03-REQ-016.note: Non-transferability folded into the single credential-scoping requirement (dedupe with 03-REQ-057).

03-REQ-017.note: Resolution of a credential to its issuing team is the observable precondition of scope enforcement and retires onto the scoping requirement; the identity-kind-exposure helper shape (resolveIdentity) is code mechanism — rationale in this change's design.md.

03-REQ-019.note: Instances admit only Convex-issued signed tokens; the RS256/OIDC naming is mechanism (crypto-neutrality, 03-REVIEW-001). Dedupe with 03-REQ-037, 05-REQ-035, 04-REQ-023.

03-REQ-020.note: Game binding, role/identity subject, expiry — re-authored crypto-neutrally (claim names are mechanism per 03-REVIEW-001).

03-REQ-021.note: Validation at connect only; expiry never disconnects an established connection (03-REVIEW-004).

03-REQ-022.note: Dedupe with 05-REQ-034a — one cross-runtime discovery contract; OIDC/JWKS endpoint shapes are mechanism.

03-REQ-023.note: Rejection criteria (signature, game binding, expiry, unregistered team). Dedupe with 04-REQ-018/022.

03-REQ-024.note: Operator tokens require membership per the game's roster snapshot (see roster-snapshot-binding).

03-REQ-025.note: Bot tokens require a valid game credential for a registered team.

03-REQ-026.note: Issuance floor owned here; spectator eligibility policy beyond authentication and consumption semantics live with the live-game-observation story (03-REVIEW-009 routed there).

03-REQ-026a.note: Coach-token issuance owned here, mirroring spectator tokens; seam — consumption semantics (the filtered read-only live view) live with the live-game-observation story, and coach designation with team-management.

03-REQ-027.note: Two-hour lifetime, refresh without re-auth, teardown as the real boundary (03-REVIEW-004).

03-REQ-028.note: A team's admitted human or bot connection may stage for any team snake; the 'staging discipline is last-write-wins within the instance' clause re-authors as effective-move semantics over the append-only log (operator-control/staged-move-log).

03-REQ-029.note: Action half (no staging/altering for another team) was already carried by the requirement body and #staging-is-team-checked; this change adds the observation half (filtered-view-only cross-team reads; spectators see no private state, stage nothing). Invisibility-filter mechanics stay with live-game-observation.

03-REQ-030.note: Map-entry-only: existing text already states SpacetimeDB holds no notion of individual operators and Convex is sole authority for the operator-to-snake mapping. Staging attribution records (Agent values SpacetimeDB never interprets) are replay-and-audit's story, not an authority record.

03-REQ-031.note: Every admitted connection subject to the invisibility filter; spectators filtered on opponent terms — dedupe with 02-REQ-010 and 04-REQ-047 into the single filtering requirement.

03-REQ-032.note: stagedBy holds an Agent value resolved from the connection's credential at connect time; the game runtime never interprets or branches on it (dedupe with 04-REQ-020/039; connect-time model per superseding 04-REVIEW-011).

03-REQ-033.note: HTTP API authorized by bearer API keys; admin-only creation (per resolved 05-REVIEW-004), revocable. The revocation means retires with 05-REQ-052 onto key-management.

03-REQ-034.note: Unrecoverable (one-way digest) storage; plaintext disclosed exactly once at creation. Dedupe cluster with 05-REQ-046 and 08-REQ-095b/095c; hash algorithm and key format are mechanism.

03-REQ-035.note: Key bound permanently to the creating admin; global scope. Dedupe with 05-REQ-047; the scope's consistency with the strictly observational admin role is carried by key-capability-bounds (see design.md).

03-REQ-036.note: Keys never authorize identity creation or actions requiring interactive OAuth. Dedupe with 05-REQ-048.

03-REQ-037.note: Convex sole issuer of all cross-runtime credentials (dedupe with 03-REQ-019, 05-REQ-035).

03-REQ-038.note: Team identifiers seeded into a game instance denote exactly one persistent Convex team record for the game's lifetime; cross-game collision immunity follows from global-invariants/spacetimedb-instance-isolation. Matrix alt (code-mechanism) declined: falsifiable cross-runtime behaviour, kept as spec.

03-REQ-039.note: Snapshot binds in-game authorization for the whole game; seam — snapshot storage/orchestration (05-REQ-029) is game-lifecycle's, the roster mutation-freeze (03-REQ-046) is team-management's.

03-REQ-040.note: Code mechanism: maintaining game-credential signing material inside Convex without external key infrastructure. Behavioural residue is covered by identity-and-authorization/sole-credential-issuer, signing-independence, and verification-without-shared-secrets; key custody/format rationale in the minting change's design.md.

03-REQ-041.note: Deliberate architectural invariant (03-REVIEW-007) re-authored crypto-neutrally — scheme names (Ed25519/RS256) are mechanism.

03-REQ-043.note: Credential/key material only to intended holders over authenticated channels; token-signing private keys never leave Convex (public verification keys only); per-team game credentials travel only via the game invitation to the nominated Server.

03-REQ-044.note: Agent resolved at each client_connected; attribution record retained for the full game including closed/replaced connections so historical stagedBy values stay consistent (dedupe with 04-REQ-020/021).

03-REQ-045.note: Persisted game record carries Agent values only, never raw connection Identities; no resolution pass at serialization (dedupe with 05-REQ-042).

03-REQ-046.note: Roster mutations (member add/remove, captaincy, server-nomination changes) rejected while a team's game is playing (dedupe with 05-REQ-013; 03-REVIEW-006 hard-rejection semantics).

03-REQ-047.note: Per-game authorization snapshot is append-only historical fact: post-game roster edits never erase attribution; persisted attribution binds to the snapshot (identity-and-authorization/roster-snapshot-binding), never to current membership.

03-REQ-048.note: Convex authenticates itself for provisioning, initialization, finish-notification registration, retrieval, and teardown; no other party can invoke the privileged instance operations. The concrete self-issued-JWT/management-API mechanism stays in code.

03-REQ-049.note: Servers have no persistent platform identity; the domain is team configuration. The credential-scoping half (team+game scope, none at rest, expiry) is carried by identity-and-authorization/game-credential-scope and global-invariants/ephemeral-game-credentials (gi-dedupe checked — no re-authoring).

03-REQ-050.note: At game start the platform sends a game invitation to each participating team's nominated server. Where the send sits inside start orchestration (after config freeze and instance provisioning) is the game-lifecycle story's.

03-REQ-051.note: Delivery via HTTPS POST to a single well-known invitation endpoint path fixed platform-wide; the literal path is code-level, its stability spec'd via forkable-reference-app's enumerated surface.

03-REQ-052.note: DNS resolution of the nominated domain is the sufficient ownership proof; the platform sends to the domain and receives no credentials from it, so no challenge is performed.

03-REQ-053.note: The invitation carries the per-team game credential plus the game context needed to act on it; what the credential grants is identity-and-authorization/game-credential-scope.

03-REQ-054.note: The server must accept for its team to proceed; the reference implementation auto-accepts by default. Refusal consequences for the game (03-REQ-056) are the game-lifecycle story's.

03-REQ-055.note: Custom servers may reject; reference implementation offers a server-side whitelist, default unrestricted. The legacy 'by player email or Centaur Team ID' key enumeration is authored abstractly pending this change's Open Question (email keys collide with the email-free invitation payload).

03-REQ-056.note: Invitation-refusal branching: non-tournament refusal/timeout aborts (game stays not-started, error names the servers); the tournament arm is authored as an abstract competition-format override with forfeited seats and the walkover below the two-team minimum. Forfeit/walkover SCORING (the 1.0/0/no-contest ladder) is the tournaments story's, per the author routing with 05-REQ-027a.

03-REQ-058.note: Credential lifetime bounded to the game; enforced per request by live-game-issuance (05-REVIEW-012).

03-REQ-059.note: Exactly two grants: team-scoped Centaur-subsystem writes and bot-token requests.

03-REQ-062.note: See all games across teams (dedupe with 05-REQ-066).

03-REQ-063.note: Watch any replay; implicit coach of every team (dedupe with 05-REQ-066).

03-REQ-064.note: Code mechanism (dedupe with 05-REQ-068): how admin accounts are designated is deliberately unspecified; the observable contract (platform-level designation on the user record, effective without reload) is identity-and-authorization/platform-admin-role.

03-REQ-065.note: Map-entry-only: the existing requirement already states read access follows Google identity and never the Server visited.

03-REQ-066.note: Every server serves the same UI and the user sees the same data regardless of server visited; the server is an open-source client. The identity-not-server read-access principle itself (03-REQ-065) is global-invariants'.

03-REQ-067.note: Accepted-exfiltration residue: a malicious Snek Centaur Server can inject client-side code exfiltrating data its visitors can read — an accepted trust trade-off (visit only Servers you trust), not a falsifiable behaviour. Retired here as the enforcement-model requirement it qualifies; the user-facing trust prose lands in team-server-management's Purpose (authored by that change).

04-REQ-001.note: dropped: module-boundary meta obsoleted by capability carving

04-REQ-002.note: Game runtime holds/derives/exposes no platform-wide persistent state; instance state is single-game-scoped and dies with the instance (teardown-after-export bracket owned by game-lifecycle).

04-REQ-003.note: No gameplay mutation from unadmitted connections.

04-REQ-004.note: Historical record with every completed turn's full observable state directly queryable, no re-execution, no other runtime (satisfied 02-REQ-013).

04-REQ-005.note: Historical record append-only once committed; sole exception the one-time destroyedTurn stamp (dedupe cluster with 04-REQ-059/066 and 06-REQ-039).

04-REQ-006.note: Per-turn snapshot of each snake's full state at each turn boundary — part of the record-contents enumeration.

04-REQ-007.note: Item lifetimes tracked by spawn/destroyed turn; any turn's item set directly derivable; game-start items spawn turn 0.

04-REQ-008.note: Board layout written once at initialization, never modified after — part of the record-contents enumeration.

04-REQ-009.note: Per-turn record of each team's post-turn budget and declaration kind (explicit with timestamp vs clock expiry; turn-pacing/turn-declaration owns the declaration semantics).

04-REQ-010.note: Per-turn wall-clock start and resolution-start moments recorded; resolution never assumed instantaneous.

04-REQ-011.note: All turn events retained, attributed to their producing turn; within-turn ordering carried by the canonical representation order (replay-and-audit/canonical-event-order).

04-REQ-012.note: Record sufficient for a complete replay — every board state, item lifetime, turn event, stagedBy attribution — without any other runtime (dedupe with 02-REQ-013/014).

04-REQ-013.note: Privileged once-only initialization by the authenticated platform orchestration, delivering precomputed starting state, dynamic gameplay parameters, roster snapshot, game identifier, and the always-forwarded root seed. Payload field shapes stay in code.

04-REQ-014.note: Successful initialization leaves turn 0 fully written (board, snake snapshots, initial items, budgets, identifier, roster available to admission).

04-REQ-015.note: Post-initialization the instance is ready for connections, staging, and declarations — the ready-after half of the once-only contract.

04-REQ-016.note: Re-initialization rejected; pre-init gameplay operations rejected; pre-init clients disconnected; no clock runs before initialization completes.

04-REQ-017.note: The runtime never generates a board (that half is carried by game-configuration/generation-parameter-boundary); it validates the delivered payload's structural integrity and rejects malformed payloads synchronously. Infeasibility surfacing to the configuring user is game-configuration/infeasibility-surfaced.

04-REQ-018.note: Connect-time application-level validation of the token's claims; callback naming (client_connected) is mechanism. Dedupe with 03-REQ-023.

04-REQ-019.note: Game-binding/subject/roster validation and role derivation at admission; the coach-view filtering it grants is stated on coach-tokens; parse-helper detail is mechanism.

04-REQ-020.note: Participant attribution record: one Agent-mapped entry per admitted connection, retained for the instance's whole life (dedupe with 03-REQ-044; connect-time model per 04-REVIEW-011).

04-REQ-021.note: Attribution entries never mutated/deleted on connection close; reconnect yields a fresh entry; historical references remain resolvable without the original connection.

04-REQ-022.note: Disconnect on failed validation with no admission or attribution record written (constraint-mined as reject-before-touching-state).

04-REQ-023.note: Convex-issued validated tokens are the only admission mechanism (dedupe with 03-REQ-019).

04-REQ-024.note: The move-staging operation accepts (snake, direction) only from operator/bot connections of the owning team; all other callers rejected. Dedupe with 03-REQ-028 into one team-scoped-staging requirement; role-level refusal of spectators/coaches is identity-and-authorization/role-bound-privileges.

04-REQ-025.note: The append-only staged-move log: new entries never overwrite, effective move = latest entry, no cancel operation, retained for the game's lifetime, not cleared at resolution. The authoritative model that supersedes 02-REQ-011/012's wording (assignment-matrix Q4). Current-turn scoping of the effective move constraint-mined from the legacy Design's resolution read. Per-entry attribution/timestamps (04-REQ-026/027) are replay-and-audit's story.

04-REQ-026.note: Each accepted staged move permanently records writer Agent, wall-clock acceptance time, and staging turn.

04-REQ-027.note: Staged-move storage is append-only, historical, part of the permanent record; enables sub-turn reconstruction of who staged what (log structure itself owned by operator-control/staged-move-log).

04-REQ-028.note: No legality validation at staging; consequences only at resolution via the collision rules. Preserves the explore-a-direction affordance (see operator-control/board-and-move-interface#exploration-is-staging).

04-REQ-029.note: No cross-team staging even with spoofed parameters; team membership is the connection-level association from admission and cannot be asserted per call. The general team-granularity principle stays with global-invariants; this entry pins the staging-specific behaviour.

04-REQ-030.note: The chess-timer semantics implemented within the game instance's own state, no external runtime mediating clock timing. The arithmetic RULES it bound to stay in game-engine/chess-timer (cited, not restated); the carve-out-deduction correction (04-REVIEW-023 cascade) survives as the every-instant budget+clock invariant. Turn-0 clock start at playability constraint-mined from the legacy Design's init sequence.

04-REQ-031.note: The declare-turn-over operation: team-only, stops the clock and banks the remainder (game-engine/chess-timer#declaration-banks-the-remainder), idempotent within the turn, spectators rejected. The declaration-timestamp persistence clause rides the per-turn record, which is replay-and-audit's story (04-REQ-009); the kind-distinction behaviour is authored here.

04-REQ-032.note: Autonomous clock-expiry detection treated as implicit declaration, marked distinctly from explicit declaration. The record that persists the distinction is replay-and-audit's (04-REQ-009); the distinguishable-kinds behaviour is authored here.

04-REQ-033.note: Resolution triggered exactly once per turn at the moment all participating teams have declared (any mix of kinds), and by no other condition — not wall-clock time, administrative action, or connection changes.

08-REQ-044.note: SPLIT: the four-direction affordance reflecting the staged direction with lethal directions visibly discouraged but selectable is authored here; the stateMap score labels and score-consistent colouring are the decision-transparency story's substance (its decision-display requirements decorate this affordance).

08-REQ-045.note: SPLIT: direction pick immediately stages as an operator-originated move and auto-sets manual (authored here, with the ordering-race invariant); the triggered worst-case preview is the decision-transparency story's substance.

08-REQ-046.note: Staged moves freely changeable until the team's turn is over; no separate commit affordance; the interface makes exploring-equals-staging legible.

08-REQ-047.note: Manual checkbox reflects the flag, editable by the current holder only; checking locks the currently staged move (no new staging), unchecking returns the snake to automated staging. Checkbox presentation is mechanism; the lock/resume behaviour is the requirement.

08-REQ-048.note: With a held snake and a picked direction, the board additionally renders that (snake, direction)'s recorded worst-case world: current positions solid, worst-case simulated positions as translucent overlays. Read from the computed display state only. The exact visual treatment of frozen snakes is a design decision; the timestamps that enable it are guaranteed in computed-display-state#timestamps-travel-with-the-world.

08-REQ-049.note: dropped: documented MVP deferral of worst-case annotations (08-REVIEW-023); substrate excised

08-REQ-050.note: UI-mirror folded as scenario (author decision): the preview updates reactively as new snapshots publish, so a direction left picked shows the worst-case world evolving in place.

08-REQ-051.note: No preview without both a picked direction and existing computed display state — the board then shows only the current state; the interface never previews a world it has no record of.

08-REQ-052.note: The Drive-type chooser with pinned-first-then-lexicographic-by-nickname ordering; its option set is the config∩registry intersection per registry-defines-availability.

08-REQ-052a.note: Coach mode renders the full live interface read-only, reachable from where the live game is surfaced (including the spectating view).

08-REQ-052b.note: Coach mode exposes no affordance that writes on behalf of the coached team — controls hidden or visibly disabled.

08-REQ-052c.note: Coach inspection is client-local: no writes, no selection shadow, no displacement, at most one inspected snake per coach client, coexisting with operators' real selection shadows.

08-REQ-052d.note: Coach inspection visually and gesturally distinct from operator selection.

08-REQ-053.note: Targeting mode per target type: eligibility-predicate-accepted targets highlighted and confirmable, the rest dimmed and inert.

08-REQ-054.note: Deterministic nearest-first keyboard cycling through eligible targets; Escape cancels without side effects. The exact three-key sort (A*-distance, clockwise angle, identity tiebreak) is demoted to code with rationale in this change's design.md — the binding residue is determinism and nearest-first.

08-REQ-055.note: Target confirmation adds the Drive at its default weight with no additional confirmation step.

08-REQ-056.note: Active Drives listed with target and weight, weight-editable and removable, edits effective immediately via the live-editing contract.

08-REQ-057.note: UI-mirror of portfolio persistence: the interface never resets Drives, overrides, or the temperature override on selection changes or turn transitions. dedupe:06-REQ-016.

08-REQ-058.note: Negative: no affordance to add a Drive outside the configured-and-registered set; registration is a code-level concern, not a runtime UI affordance.

08-REQ-059.note: Per-direction decision breakdown for the held snake: one row per active heuristic with name, raw normalised output in the recorded worst-case world, portfolio weight, weighted contribution, and relative impact. Rendered purely from published outputs per decision-transparency/published-slots-only (the legacy no-recompute downstream note, minted as a requirement — the routed constraint-mining lead).

08-REQ-060.note: UI-mirror folded as scenarios (author decision): the breakdown updates reactively on new snapshots and on the operator switching the examined direction — the latter a pure re-read of the already-published record.

08-REQ-061.note: UI-mirror: the per-operator tempo toggle — owned exclusively by the operator it describes, reactive across all connected team members — folds into the tempo requirement (written only by that operator, toggleable at any moment). The thinking/flow vocabulary and stance semantics are carried in the requirement text.

08-REQ-062.note: The active-operator set (currently connected member operators; boot leaves it exactly as a disconnect, per operator-control/captain-boot) and the unanimous-flow PASSIVE necessary precondition on the automated player's declaration — becoming true triggers nothing; zero active operators defers automated declaration (08-REVIEW-011); Captain submit and clock expiry bypass entirely. The when-to-declare rules within the permitted window live in turn-pacing/final-flush.

08-REQ-063.note: Tempo toggleable in either direction at any time, immediately affecting the unanimity; every operator interaction remains available regardless of tempo; the team's clock runs and expires regardless of any operator's tempo (expiry declares via the engine rule, game-engine/chess-timer).

08-REQ-064.note: Every path into the active set — first join, reconnect after a drop, rejoin after a boot — sets the operator's tempo to flow as part of (re)connecting, and this is the only automatic tempo write; no per-turn reset exists. The client-issues-the-call shape is mechanism; the uniform flow-on-(re)connect behaviour is the requirement.

08-REQ-064a.note: Coaches and admins acting as implicit coaches have no tempo, are never active operators, and never count for or against the unanimity; the presence display distinguishes observers from member operators (the visual-distinction half rides the pacing-header's tempo-from-the-record presentation and operator-control's presence identity).

08-REQ-065.note: SPLIT: the Captain's turn-submit — immediate declaration with exactly the currently staged moves, regardless of any operator's tempo — is authored here, alongside the Captain-only exposure of both Captain controls; the boot affordance's own semantics were retired via operator-control/captain-boot (cited), and its quorum consequence via turn-pacing/flow-quorum.

08-REQ-066.note: The turn-submit affordance keyboard-bindable so the Captain can operate it without pointer input; the specific binding stays mechanism.

08-REQ-067.note: UI-mirror plus enforcement: non-Captains see neither Captain control, and any invocation — keyboard included — is rejected server-side at the function contract; the tempo toggle is expressly not Captain-gated (every member operator owns their own).

08-REQ-068.note: Tempo toggles, Captain boots, and Captain submissions emit their action-log events; tempo/boot are time-based (wall-clock anchored, not turn-keyed) so replay reconstructs the active-operator set and per-operator tempo at any scrubbed moment. The control affordances themselves are turn-pacing's and operator-control's.

08-REQ-068a.note: SPLIT: the boot itself — forced disconnect identical to network loss, no persistent operator state, no lockout, reconnect rejoins, Captain-only server-side — is authored here (boot-writes-no-persistent-state constraint-mined from the legacy Design). The active-set/quorum consequences of leaving and rejoining (and the tempo-flow reconnect write) are the turn-pacing story's substance.

08-REQ-069.note: Unified viewer for finished games with a persisted replay: board-level mode (turn granularity, all authenticated users) + team-perspective mode (sub-turn, participants only).

08-REQ-070.note: Board-level mode sources everything from the persisted replay, never a game instance; functional after teardown.

08-REQ-070a.note: Turn-granularity rendering: terrain, snakes, items, hazards, fertile tiles, per-team scoreboard (normalised score headline par 1.0, aggregate length secondary; recorded rows of live-game-observation/scoreboard-sole-aggregate-authority), visually consistent with live spectating.

08-REQ-070b.note: Per-turn event log listing the selected turn's events, matching the closed enumeration (replay-and-audit/turn-event-record).

08-REQ-071.note: Team-perspective mode presents the live operator interface read-only: mutating affordances disabled/absent, inspection affordances (direction preview, worst-case preview, decision breakdown) fully functional.

08-REQ-071a.note: Team-perspective mode offered exclusively for games the viewing human participated in as a team member — interface scoping, not data-readability narrowing.

08-REQ-072.note: Single unified timeline control governs both modes (play/pause/scrub/speed/mode toggle); at any position the viewer displays the reconstructed state there — the reconstruction union itself is replay-and-audit/experience-reconstruction.

08-REQ-072a.note: Per-Turn/Timeline mode toggle; mode and speed client-local, restored within the session, never persisted to the platform; default Per-Turn.

08-REQ-072b.note: Per-Turn mode: equidistant turn ticks, snapping to end-of-turn states, playback in turns/second. The literal speed set is mechanism, demoted to code (design.md, timeline-literals section).

08-REQ-072c.note: Timeline mode: real wall-clock axis, turn markers at actual declaration times with proportional spacing, continuous scrub, playback at multiples of real time, mode-unit speed labels. Literal speed set demoted to code (design.md).

08-REQ-072d.note: Keyboard scrubbing matches the active mode's granularity (whole turns; fine time steps and turn-marker jumps). Exact key/modifier bindings are mechanism, demoted to code (design.md).

08-REQ-073.note: Historical selections render as coloured shadows in the original per-operator live-play colours; unconnected-at-that-moment operators produce no shadow.

08-REQ-074.note: Inspection is purely client-local: at most one inspected snake per client, no writes anywhere, no shadow, never displaces reconstructed selections (resolved 08-REVIEW-008).

08-REQ-075.note: Any snake on the viewed team inspectable at any scrubbed moment regardless of who historically held it.

08-REQ-075a.note: Team-perspective replay reveals nothing about opponents beyond the team's filtered view at the original time (live-game-observation/invisibility-filtering); invisible-then stays invisible-now in that mode.

08-REQ-075b.note: Board-level mode displays nothing derived from the action log: no selections, shadows, stateMap/worst-case/heuristic data.

08-REQ-075c.note: Direct-link affordance: a URL identifying the game takes any authenticated user straight to its replay viewer.

08-REQ-076.note: Code mechanism (author-resolved demotion): the data-source abstraction binding UI reads to live subscriptions vs persisted replay + action log. The fork-stability contract of the exported surface was authored by team-server-management (forkable-reference-app); the behavioural property of the replay binding is minted as replay-and-audit/replay-binding-mutation-free. Rationale in this change's design.md.

08-REQ-077.note: Code mechanism (author-resolved demotion): components not distinguishing live from replayed state is client architecture. Its observable core — read-only-ness comes from the binding offering no writes, never per-component branches — is replay-and-audit/replay-binding-mutation-free#read-only-is-not-per-component. Rationale in this change's design.md.

08-REQ-078.note: The replay binding exposes no mutation surface at all — structural absence, not runtime refusal; authored behaviourally per the author-resolved boundary (the abstraction itself is 08-REQ-076's note-only mechanism).

08-REQ-079.note: Forkable reference repository, full source ownership; the 'no customisation relaxes invariants / enforcement is external' half is carried by global-invariants (08-REQ-080a's requirement), and the fork-stable compatibility surface is constraint-mined into the same requirement.

08-REQ-080.note: Spectating available for any playing game to every authenticated user, without team membership.

08-REQ-080a.note: No application customisation relied on for any security or correctness invariant; this change broadens the requirement from 'never the Server library' to 'never any client's presentation'.

08-REQ-081.note: Entry obtains a spectator access token via the platform's issuance path and connects under it (identity-and-authorization/spectator-tokens owns issuance).

08-REQ-081a.note: Live interface available from the playing transition until the finished transition — the availability bracket.

08-REQ-082.note: The spectating view subscribes to the live current-state pattern and renders board, items, observable snakes, and events in real time.

08-REQ-082a.note: On finish, connected operators get the terminal view — final scores and a replay link, zero mutating affordances — replacing the live interface.

08-REQ-083.note: The UI honours the server-side filter and never infers or reconstructs invisible-snake state from any channel.

08-REQ-083a.note: Subscription loss surfaced, never papered over with fabricated/stale state; resubscribe and render fresh on recovery.

08-REQ-084.note: Scoreboard sourced exclusively from the server aggregate channel, rendered exactly as delivered — dedupe with 08-REQ-084b and 04-REQ-071 (08-REVIEW-018 posture).

08-REQ-084a.note: The application persists no authoritative state of its own across sessions; all operator-visible state re-derived from the owning runtimes each session.

08-REQ-084b.note: Never compute team-level aggregates client-side from per-snake data — folded into the sole-aggregate-authority requirement.

08-REQ-085.note: Spectating displays current turn number and, per team, remaining budget and declared-turn-over status.

08-REQ-086.note: Spectating exposes zero mutating affordances — structural read-only, matching the token's terms.

08-REQ-087.note: Timeline scrubber over all completed turns; up-front full-history subscription on entry per 08-REVIEW-019; scrubbing never interrupts the live subscription.

08-REQ-088.note: Scrubbed view visibly not-live with a one-action return to the live head.

08-REQ-089.note: Subscription released and spectator token discarded on navigation away or game finish.

08-REQ-090.note: Player Profile for every user record — own profile via global navigation, others linked at minimum from member listings and game histories. The authenticated-only half (08-REVIEW-016) is authored once in accounts-and-profiles/no-public-surface.

08-REQ-091.note: Profile contents: display name, current and historical memberships, chronological game history under the historical-or-current membership rule (cited from replay-and-audit/team-game-history) — and never an email, even on self-view (the email half is owned by accounts-and-profiles/email-confidentiality).

08-REQ-091a.note: No user-facing query, view, or exported shape exposes any user's email to anyone, the user themselves included; email stored solely for identity matching and administrative operations. The legacy downstream constraint on the platform query layer is authored as behaviour here: shapes omit email at the data-contract boundary, and participation snapshots store none (constraint-mining promotion of the legacy design's projection and email-free-rosterSnapshot notes).

08-REQ-092.note: Player aggregate statistics (games played, win rate, average team score at minimum) computed from exactly the data populating the game-history listing, hence consistent with it.

08-REQ-093.note: Player-profile historical attribution resolves via participating-team snapshots, never current records — a historical game shows the team of the day even after team changes or archiving.

08-REQ-094.note: Team Profile for every Centaur Team (archived included), visible to every authenticated user; the authenticated-only half (08-REVIEW-016) is authored once in accounts-and-profiles/no-public-surface.

08-REQ-094a.note: Global leaderboard ranking teams by one of a closed criteria set: at minimum win rate (with a minimum-games qualifying threshold), total wins, and average score — authored as average normalised score, the cross-game comparable form (decision in the change's design.md).

08-REQ-094b.note: Criterion switching plus time-window filtering from a closed set (at minimum all time, last 30 days, last 7 days).

08-REQ-094c.note: Optional restriction of the ranking to games played within a single room; the ranking then considers only that subset.

08-REQ-094d.note: Each ranked entry links to that team's Team Profile view; carried in the leaderboard requirement's body.

08-REQ-094e.note: Archived teams remain in the default leaderboard under their archived identity; rankings resolve via participating-team snapshots and archiving never rewrites competitive history (08-REVIEW-017; archive semantics cited from team-management/archive-not-delete).

08-REQ-094f.note: Leaderboard exposes nothing to unauthenticated visitors; folded into the single authenticated-only surface rule (08-REVIEW-016), citing identity-and-authorization/authentication-required.

08-REQ-095.note: Team Profile contents: name, colour, captain, current members, server nomination with latest health status (record substance cited from team-management/team-record), and the team's full chronological game history visible to any authenticated user (consistent with replay-and-audit/finished-games-public).

08-REQ-095a.note: The management surface listing active and revoked keys with label and timestamps. 'Every authenticated user' superseded by the admin-only resolution of 05-REVIEW-004 — re-authored as an admin affordance (design.md).

08-REQ-095b.note: Single creation-time plaintext display with an explicit copy affordance before dismissal.

08-REQ-095c.note: Never display/store/transmit plaintext after the single disclosure — deduped with the identity capability's custody hygiene by citing identity-and-authorization/client-credential-custody (whose one-time-disclosure exception this is) rather than restating it.

08-REQ-095d.note: Scope communication at the management surface, specialized to the admin-only model: a key carries its creator's global admin authority and stops working if the creator loses the role (per-request re-check in admin-api-keys#creator-no-longer-admin).

08-REQ-096.note: Team aggregate statistics: games played, win rate, average score, and a head-to-head record against every team ever played, derived from the same data as the history listing.

08-REQ-096a.note: Admin experience enumeration (team/game/replay visibility, live coach entry) — UI mirror of the role's read breadth.

08-REQ-096b.note: Admin strictly read-only over game and Centaur-subsystem state (constraint-mined as admin-extends-read-only).

08-REQ-097.note: Head-to-head opponent attribution resolves via participating-team snapshots and is stable under opponent archiving (08-REVIEW-017).

08-REQ-098.note: UI mirror folded as a scenario: the Team Profile exposes no mutating affordance over team state; mutation authority lives with team-management (the profile at most links onward to team-management/team-management-view).

08-REQ-100.note: Server-side invariant rejections surfaced as explicit, user-legible feedback at the point of action; never silently swallowed.

08-REQ-101.note: Invariant-gated affordance enablement derives from server-held state, not client optimism; where not yet derivable, dispatch anyway and surface the authoritative result.

08-REQ-102.note: under config-on-the-game there are no room defaults; the rule survives as views-read-the-games-own-record plus the launch freeze

08-REQ-103.note: Views referencing archived teams render from the persisted participating-team snapshot and present archived status explicitly; never broken references. Matrix alt (accounts-and-profiles) declined: binds every historical view across capabilities.

08-REQ-104.note: Every mutating action from any surface dispatched against the owning runtime's server-side contract under identical invariants; no UI bypass; rejection surfacing pinned by global-invariants/client-truthfulness#rejections-reach-the-user. Integrator-facing parity half (05-REQ-050) is platform-integrations' story.

02-REVIEW-003.note: Provisioning is tied to game start, not record creation: auto-created successors are unstarted, instance-less, and get a fresh instance only at their own launch — uniform across every creation path.

02-REVIEW-005.note: WebSocket named-transport question resolved as: transport is mechanism and stays out of requirements; the behavioural residue — the direct, independently authenticated dual-connection topology — is authored in operator-control/operator-dual-connection.

02-REVIEW-006.note: The retrieval pattern was deliberately left open at the architectural level; the invariant kept is persistence-before-teardown. Later resolved concretely as bundling into the finish notification (05-REVIEW-015), reflected in finish-notification.

02-REVIEW-007.note: Spectator intersection semantics: spectators see no invisible snake of any team — treated as opponents of every team.

02-REVIEW-008.note: The lifecycle has a second path out of not-started: an override-governed launch seating fewer than the minimum walks straight to finished without entering playing, with any provisioned instance torn down and the initialization roster restricted to accepting teams on the proceeding path.

03-REVIEW-001.note: Crypto-neutrality upheld in the re-authoring: the minted requirements name no cryptographic primitives (JWT/HMAC/JWKS/RSA); primitives are mechanism, with the policy recorded in the minting change's design.md.

03-REVIEW-002.note: Google specifically is binding, not an implementation choice — supporting another provider requires deliberate spec revision.

03-REVIEW-003.note: Email canonical; provider email change forks a new identity and the prior identity keeps its history unmigrated.

03-REVIEW-004.note: Two-hour token lifetime with connect-time-only validation; instance teardown, not expiry, is the primary post-game boundary.

03-REVIEW-005.note: stagedBy attribution granularity: originally resolved as opaque in-instance Identity with export-time resolution; superseded (with 04-REVIEW-011) by connect-time Agent resolution — stagedBy carries Agent | null from the start, the runtime never interprets it, and persisted records contain no raw Identities.

03-REVIEW-006.note: Mid-game roster edits hard-rejected (option A strengthened) — the freeze keeps the live record coherent with the binding snapshot; the snapshot-binding half lives in identity-and-authorization/roster-snapshot-binding and the append-only-attribution half with the replay-and-audit story.

03-REVIEW-007.note: Independence-of-compromise between credential-type signing materials kept as a requirement, stated crypto-neutrally.

03-REVIEW-008.note: Option A: healthchecks are unauthenticated, verifying reachability only — keeping the sole-issuer and no-secret-at-nomination rules exception-free. The minimal-payload guidance is elevated to the scenario's violation clause: adding team-scoped state to the response changes the threat model and requires revisiting the decision.

03-REVIEW-009.note: Spectator eligibility policy (private games, visibility, rate limits) deliberately deferred; the scenario records that authentication + game-in-play are the only gates until the requirement is deliberately revised.

03-REVIEW-012.note: Forfeit-on-invitation-refusal branches for tournament rounds: two-plus acceptances play (forfeiters marked, score 0), exactly one is a walkover (sole acceptor 1.0), zero is a no-contest (no winner) — forfeits distinguishable from played losses by the unseated-teams marking. The non-tournament abort arm and the abstract format-override hook are game-lifecycle/launch-gates (03-REQ-056, mapped by migrate-game-lifecycle); this capability is the concrete format.

04-REVIEW-002.note: Fallback representation: stagedBy is nullable and null denotes exactly a fallback-determined move — no sentinel value, no split event kinds.

04-REVIEW-003.note: Hazard-damage event dedup: survivors emit hazard-damage events; snakes that die with hazard contributing emit only the death event with hazard among its sources — no double counting.

04-REVIEW-004.note: Intra-turn ordering: events are a set; the canonical representation order (type class, then subject id) is deterministic, derived from event data, and expresses no causal/temporal relation.

04-REVIEW-006.note: Option A: game-end rejection begins at the commit of the end-detecting turn; in-flight operations arriving after the commit are rejected as game-over — no grace window between commit and enforcement.

04-REVIEW-007.note: Unbounded in-instance retention for the instance's life (Option A): the one-pass end-of-game persistence contract forbids any runtime-side retention cap; post-teardown retention is the platform's concern.

04-REVIEW-009.note: No subscription delivery-order guarantee: a turn's events are a set; deterministic order is read from the stored canonical representation order.

04-REVIEW-011.note: Connect-time Agent resolution: connection identity resolved to an Agent at admission (not at export); attribution entries append-only across reconnections so historical stagedBy references resolve without the original connection.

04-REVIEW-012.note: Turn-0 starting positions and initial food are fully public; the filter encodes invisibility only, never pre-game positional privacy.

04-REVIEW-013.note: Seed accessibility (Option B): the per-game seed is hidden from every game client during play but included in the replay export, making deterministic reproducibility externally verifiable.

04-REVIEW-014.note: No final-submission barrier: a pre-declaration burst of staged moves is ordinary appends, and resolution consumes exactly what the log holds at the instant of declaration; submission-pass coordination is entirely a team-side concern.

04-REVIEW-020.note: Scoreboard computed over the true alive set including invisible snakes; eliminated teams zero-filled, never omitted.

04-REVIEW-021.note: Live team score is the normalised as-if-ended score (par 1.0) per game-engine/scoring, decoupled from aggregate length.

04-REVIEW-023.note: The budget carve-out correction cascade: the per-turn clock is deducted from the budget the moment it is carved out, so budget + clock = total remaining time at every instant and depletion is reachable. Promoted from boundary arithmetic to the every-instant invariant in the realization requirement; the arithmetic rule itself lives in game-engine/chess-timer#carve-out-arithmetic.

05-REVIEW-002.note: Rooms cannot be deleted — archive-only, hidden from listings while preserving all historical games, replays, and action logs; unarchivable.

05-REVIEW-003.note: Option B — tournament-wide freeze from first-round start to final-round end; inter-round interludes remain frozen. The later decided source for the whole-event question the 03-REVIEW-006 sub-question left open; carried into the delta per this change's Open Question 1 pending author confirmation.

05-REVIEW-005.note: Option A: only game_start, firing at the not-started -> playing transition. No game_created or game_will_start event — subscribers first hear of a game at launch, when its configuration is frozen and actionable (#start-payload-is-the-played-config carries the rationale behaviourally).

05-REVIEW-007.note: Readiness lives on the game record from eager creation onward; Captain-only declaration; cleared whenever a new game is auto-created — re-authored as every fresh game beginning with no team ready.

05-REVIEW-008.note: config-on-game architecture; the auto-create/room-edit race is eliminated structurally (no room-level config); successor-creation mechanics belong to the game-lifecycle story

05-REVIEW-009.note: Differentiated healthcheck behaviour at start: manual games blocked (with the failing teams identified) until all servers pass; schedule-bound competition starts ignore health, and a team whose server is not running in time is simply absent.

05-REVIEW-011.note: Team deletion disallowed; archive flag hides from listings and new enrolments while preserving all live and historical state.

05-REVIEW-012.note: Game credential effectively dies at game finish via per-request status re-check; the two-hour expiry is a leak backstop, not the access boundary.

05-REVIEW-013.note: The invitation response window lives on the invitation contract (team-server-management), which this capability cites without restating a number. NOTE: this review decided ten seconds while the sibling change authored thirty (from the module-03 Design) — flagged as this change's Open Question 1; finalize this note with the human resolution.

05-REVIEW-014.note: Timekeeper role eliminated; captaincy enforced structurally via the team record's single captain reference; membership carries no role information — every member is an Operator.

05-REVIEW-015.note: Callback credential validated statelessly (self-contained proof, never persisted, no stored-copy comparison) and the complete record bundled into the finish notification, enabling immediate teardown within the same terminal handling.

05-REVIEW-016.note: Orchestration reordering for forfeit semantics: the initialization-deferred-until-invitations-resolve ordering is owned by game-lifecycle/launch-orchestration#invitations-resolve-before-init and the walkover status transition by game-lifecycle/status-authority#walkover-straight-to-finished (both mapped by migrate-game-lifecycle); this capability owns the tournament-side resolution ladder and that round chaining proceeds from any branch.

05-REVIEW-018.note: Walkover score value: sole acceptor records 1.0 — par, the normalised-scoring value for a field of one — forfeiters 0; the 'winner-by-default' sentinel removed everywhere. Phrased abstractly in the delta because game-engine is outside the tournaments dependency ceiling; the formula stays single-sourced in the engine's scoring requirements.

05-REVIEW-019.note: Scale-to-zero host with warm-up dispatched on game-configuration creation to amortise the cold start off the launch path; best-effort, with the launch-time cold start remaining a handled fallback. Hosting target named only in legacy design text — stays out of the spec.

06-REVIEW-001.note: Per-team is authoritative (Option A): heuristic defaults are per-CentaurTeam; a replacement server inherits them; the server's build-time registry determines availability only.

06-REVIEW-002.note: Strict team-scoping of team-private live state; cross-team reads denied; admins read as implicit coaches, designated coaches on member terms.

06-REVIEW-003.note: Each actor writes its own action-log entries under its own credential (Option A): operators directly, the server via its game credential; no brokering of operator mutations through the server; state change and entry share one transaction.

06-REVIEW-004.note: Delivery guarantees (Option C): transactional pairing — a dropped entry implies the mutation did not happen; move_staged removed from the action log entirely, recorded only in the game instance's staged-move log where act and record inherently coincide.

06-REVIEW-005.note: Temperature override persists across turns and deselection, symmetric with Drive and weight overrides (Option A).

06-REVIEW-006.note: Selection-state lifetime (Option B): live selection records cleared at game end (operator-control/exclusive-selection#cleared-at-finish); replay reconstructs selection history exclusively from the action log.

06-REVIEW-008.note: Live game-scoped record over action-log derivation for current pacing values — readers consult the record directly, never scan the log. The 'operator mode' the original decision stored was later superseded twice (ready-state, then per-operator tempo, per the module-08 review chain); the surviving substance is the live-record principle, authored for the timing fields here (the tempo record has its own requirement, turn-pacing/operator-tempo).

07-REVIEW-002.note: A manual teammate's staged move outside the evaluator's interest map adds no explored alternative — the teammate is frozen in place, consistent with the standard lattice-membership rules.

07-REVIEW-003.note: Temporal head-start compensation: consumers of simulated boards seed frozen snakes ahead proportional to their staleness, compensating for the frozen-in-place fiction.

07-REVIEW-005.note: The deadline is computed dynamically from live values (allocation vs observed remaining time, less the threshold), not from any fixed wall-clock signal; manual Captain submission suppresses the final flush (human discretion stands, no post-decision rolls) while the automatic deadline path always flushes; the scheduled cadence runs until the turn is declared over. The turn-0 allocation carve-out was removed within the legacy review chain itself.

07-REVIEW-006.note: Sampling over a partial stateMap is legitimate anytime behaviour: undefined entries are excluded, and a decision from the evaluated subset beats no decision.

07-REVIEW-007.note: Drive retirement is anchored to the turn's observed authoritative board only — satisfaction in simulated worlds contributes terminal reward but never retires.

07-REVIEW-008.note: A SpacetimeDB reconnection that resurfaces the current turn number keeps the evaluation cache; only an observed turn-number change clears it.

07-REVIEW-009.note: Staged moves are mediated exclusively by the game's SpacetimeDB instance — operators stage directly under their own tokens, and every consumer (including the team's automated player) observes staged moves from that single log; no secondary staging store or brokered path exists.

07-REVIEW-012.note: Submission interval and imminent threshold are team-tunable bot parameters (the promotion the legacy 06-REQ-011 text adopted), not framework constants; their consumption semantics stay with the pacing story.

07-REVIEW-013.note: Worst-case-world tie-breaking resolved as deterministic (Option A, lexicographic foreign-tuple order). Authored at intent grain: the requirement pins that recorded worst-case selection is deterministic — identical evaluation state always records the identical world, keeping the preview reproducible from the snapshot alone (the routed constraint-mining lead); the specific lexicographic rule is mechanism, cited from code with a // design: reference to this change.

07-REVIEW-014.note: The annotations subsystem is excised (with 08-REQ-049's MVP deferral dropped by the sibling that owns it); the surviving posture is authored here: the published slots are the complete operator-visible decision surface, heuristic contract violations are diagnosed in the hosting server's process log only with no operator-UI surface (aligning with bot-framework/author-fault-containment), and the review's restored per-snake turn timestamps travel inside the record (computed-display-state#timestamps-travel-with-the-world). A replacement annotations design remains future work extending this capability.

08-REVIEW-001.note: The load-bearing authorization split: team-scoped defaults (heuristic defaults, bot parameters) are Captain-only; game-scoped per-snake overrides (Drives, weights, activation, temperature) are editable by any current member. No timekeeper role.

08-REVIEW-002.note: Launch snapshot: all team-scoped defaults take effect only when game-scoped state is created at game start; running games are unaffected by default edits.

08-REVIEW-003.note: Game-history visibility: listing eligibility is historical-or-current membership; replay access is fully public for finished games — all teams' within-turn data readable by any authenticated user, listing scope governing discovery only; private games eliminated.

08-REVIEW-008.note: Replay inspection is purely client-local (Option A) — no shared state, so concurrent inspectors never conflict; the exclusive-lock selection mechanic is deliberately not reused for replay viewing.

08-REVIEW-011.note: The resolution that dissolved the team-level Automatic/Centaur operator-mode model into per-operator coordination: the precondition is passive, zero active operators defers automated declaration (the clock running on, expiry as backstop), and the Captain's submit and clock expiry bypass the precondition entirely. Its interim ready-state model was itself superseded by the durable tempo model (08-REVIEW-024); this change authors only the surviving tempo-model behaviours. 08-REQ-034's removal (number reserved) originates here.

08-REVIEW-013.note: Ready/unready is Captain-only (option A), matching the upstream captain-only authorization of 05-REVIEW-007; readiness read-only for non-Captains.

08-REVIEW-014.note: single generation authority: platform-side shared engine only — no client-side and no runtime-side board generation

08-REVIEW-015.note: persistence on every regeneration onto the game record; boardPreviewLocked governs launch reuse; unlocked regeneration hidden from configuration-mode views

08-REVIEW-016.note: Profiles and leaderboard are authenticated-only ('public' means any authenticated user, never the internet), and emails are never exposed by any user-facing query — not even to the user on their own profile; email stays stored solely for identity matching and administrative operations.

08-REVIEW-017.note: Deletion is not a thing on this platform: archived teams persist in the default leaderboard under their archived identity, and archiving never rewrites history, statistics, or rankings (upstream resolution carried by team-management/archive-not-delete).

08-REVIEW-018.note: Clients are dumb readers: server-side aggregate view is the sole scoreboard source; client-side aggregation of per-snake data is prohibited (would leak/under-count under invisibility).

08-REVIEW-019.note: Up-front full-history subscription on spectating entry; bounded entry latency accepted; no lazy-fetch state machine.

08-REVIEW-020.note: No platform-wide live-games listing (option A): the Room Browser's playing indicator is the general discovery path; user-scoped surfaces list only games involving the user's own teams (the home view's narrow scope itself is owned by the accounts-and-profiles story).

08-REVIEW-021.note: Registry drift handling: operable set = config ∩ build-time registry; stale config rows retained, greyed, captain-deletable, never in the in-game dropdown; new registry entries adopted via the insert-only lazy sync on a Captain visit, never overwriting Captain-edited values; the framework never writes heuristic config.

08-REVIEW-024.note: The durable per-operator tempo correction over the per-turn ready-state model: tempo persists across turns (routine turns cost zero operator actions), flow is written on every (re)connect as the only automatic write, and the Captain's boot is a stateless forced disconnect whose subject leaves the active set like any disconnect and rejoins in flow. The boot act itself was retired via operator-control/captain-boot; the quorum/rejoin consequences are authored here.
